seoTitle: Make an Account Active or Inactive in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Learn how to activate or deactivate an account in QuickBooks Online to keep your chart of accounts organized without deleting historical data.
intro: As your business changes, some accounts in your chart of accounts stop being useful — but deleting them outright can break historical reports. This guide shows how to make an account active or inactive in QuickBooks Online instead, keeping your books tidy while preserving past data. It's useful for bookkeepers and business owners cleaning up their chart of accounts.

steps.heading: Access account settings
steps.body: Go to the chart of accounts within QuickBooks Online to view the full list of active and inactive accounts.

steps.heading: Locate the account to update
steps.body: Find the specific account you want to make active or inactive from the list.

steps.heading: Make the account inactive
steps.body: Select the option to deactivate the account, which hides it from regular use without deleting its historical transactions.

steps.heading: Reactivate an account if needed
steps.body: If you need to use an account again later, locate it among inactive accounts and reactivate it in a few clicks.

steps.heading: Review your chart of accounts periodically
steps.body: Regularly clean up unused accounts to keep reports and dropdowns easier to navigate as your business evolves.

faq.question: Does making an account inactive delete its history?
faq.answer: No, historical transactions remain intact — the account is just hidden from active use going forward.

faq.question: Can I reactivate an account after making it inactive?
faq.answer: Yes, inactive accounts can be reactivated at any time from the chart of accounts list.

faq.question: Why would a business need to deactivate accounts?
faq.answer: Businesses often deactivate accounts that are no longer used, seasonal, or duplicated to keep their chart of accounts cleaner.

faq.question: Will deactivating an account affect past financial reports?
faq.answer: No, past reports will still reflect the account's historical activity even after it's marked inactive.
